text: The utilisation of robotic technology in urology has changed the landscape of radical surgery for localised prostate cancer. Early recognition of the benefits to the functional and oncological outcomes provided by three-D HD vision and precision instruments has led to the widespread adoption of robotic techniques for radical prostatectomy.

In this review, we describe the evolution of robotic-assisted radical prostatectomy, initially as it developed from skills learned from open and laparoscopic techniques, and then how it progressed alongside the advances in robotic technologies. The limitations and disadvantages of robotic prostate surgery are also discussed, and finally we explore the future of robotic technologies in urology.
